title: Job Search Automation System
taglineHtml: Criteria-first job screening workflow. OpenClaw was the early experiment; n8n is the verified rebuild.
valueHtml: Turns messy job listings into a structured, criteria-based review workflow.
problemHtml: Job listings arrive messy and inconsistent, making it easy to miss required details or apply bias while screening.
workflowHtml: I built a real n8n workflow that validates required fields, then scores listings against location, language and fit rules before generating a report.
resultShortHtml: Every listing — passed, rejected or incomplete — stays visible for human decision; the system never auto-applies.
whatItProvesHtml: I can turn messy job listings into structured, criteria-based review output.
productionSignalHtml: Real n8n execution · validation rules · review report generated · edge case caught
keyLabel: mock listings screened · 4 review states · 0 silent drops
goalHtml:
• Make job-opportunity review more consistent.
  • Check explicit criteria before judgment.
  • Flag missing data, location mismatch and language risk.
  • Produce a review report for human decision.
  • Avoid automatic applications or hidden rejection.
methodHtml:
• Start from messy job listings.
  • Validate required fields before scoring.
  • Use rules to structure location, language and basic fit.
  • Let the workflow explain the review state.
  • Keep the final apply / reject decision manual.
resultHtml:
• Explored the first job-screening logic in OpenClaw.
  • Rebuilt the useful logic as a verified n8n workflow.
  • Ran the workflow and generated a structured report.
  • Caught an incomplete listing / validation edge case.
  • Kept rejected and incomplete items visible.
failureHandledHtml:
• OpenClaw is shown as early exploration, not a finished product.
  • The rebuild removed unreliable parts and kept the useful filter logic.
  • Missing data is flagged instead of guessed.
  • Nothing is silently dropped from the report.
decisionHtml:
• AI and workflow assist with structure and review.
  • Human decision stays final.
  • The system does not auto-apply.
  • The report supports judgment; it does not replace it.
limitationHtml:
• Uses controlled / mock listing data.
  • Current rules focus on location, language and missing data.
  • It is not yet a full job-fit evaluator.
  • Human review is still required.
stagesLabel: Workflow — validate before score, human decides

stages.title: Manual trigger
stages.body: Runs on demand from n8n or CLI; no silent background run.
stages.caption: Manual trigger in the verified n8n workflow.

stages.title: Load listings
stages.body: Loads one item per job listing so the source data stays visible.
stages.caption: Raw listing data entering the workflow.

stages.title: Validate, score and flag
stages.body: Checks required fields first, then applies location, language-risk and fit rules.
stages.caption: Validation and rule-based scoring logic.

stages.title: Build summary report
stages.body: Generates a readable report where passed, rejected and incomplete listings remain visible.
stages.caption: Final report generated by the workflow.

transferItems: Keeps rejected and incomplete items visible for review.

title: Investment Reasoning Learning Database
taglineHtml: Source-first database for learning investment reasoning through evidence, risk and invalidation.
valueHtml: Separates facts, assumptions, missing data and risk before any human decision.
problemHtml: Expert investment write-ups mix facts, assumptions and conclusions together, making it hard to learn the reasoning behind them.
workflowHtml: I built a source-first database that separates evidence from interpretation, then extracts the gates, assumptions, risks and invalidation logic behind each case.
resultShortHtml: This is not a stock-picking system — it turns learning into reviewable criteria, with human judgment owning the final call.
whatItProvesHtml: I can learn expert reasoning structure instead of copying final opinions.
productionSignalHtml: Source-first database · evidence gates · assumptions / risks / invalidation extracted · reusable skill logic
keyLabel: traceable facts · 113 sources · 13 written research skills
goalHtml:
• Learn how investment reasoning works, not only what conclusion someone reaches.
  • Break expert examples into evidence, assumptions, gates, risks and invalidation.
  • Convert repeated reasoning patterns into reusable review logic.
  • Avoid copying expert opinions as automatic answers.
  • Build a source-first learning database.
methodHtml:
• Start from source material: filings, articles, interviews, notes and discussions.
  • Separate facts from interpretation and hypothesis.
  • Extract the decision structure behind the reasoning.
  • Identify review gates: evidence quality, risk, valuation, data gaps and invalidation.
  • Human review decides whether a pattern becomes reusable knowledge.
resultHtml:
• Built a large source-first research and learning database.
  • Structured professional reasoning into reusable learning material.
  • Created review gates for evidence, assumptions, risk and invalidation.
  • Used flagged mistakes as candidates for future skill patches.
  • Kept Notion as a view layer, not the source of truth.
failureHandledHtml:
• Prevents copying someone else’s conclusion without understanding the logic.
  • Prevents expert confidence from becoming automatic action.
  • Flags missing data, weak comparison and overconfident conclusions.
  • Keeps human review before any rule is promoted.
decisionHtml:
• This is not a stock-picking system.
  • It does not give investment recommendations.
  • It extracts professional reasoning structure.
  • It turns learning into review criteria.
  • Human judgment owns the final interpretation.
limitationHtml:
• Investment logic may not transfer automatically to other domains.
  • Source quality still needs human judgment.
  • A large database does not equal correctness.
  • The skill-evolution loop still needs human review.

stages.title: Source inbox
stages.body: Collect notes, filings, discussions and research examples as source material.
stages.caption: Source material entering the research database.

stages.title: Evidence tiering
stages.body: Separate source evidence from interpretation, opinion and missing information.
stages.caption: Evidence-tier grading applied to a source.

stages.title: Reasoning extraction
stages.body: Extract gates, assumptions, risks and invalidation logic — not final calls, not automatic answers.
stages.caption: Distillation output: structure, not conclusion.

stages.title: Skill card review
stages.body: Human review decides whether a pattern becomes reusable skill logic.
stages.caption: A proposed skill patch awaiting human review.

transferItems: Distils expert process without copying final opinions.
Turns repeated reasoning patterns into reviewed skill logic.

title: Source-to-Figma Data Visualization Pipeline
taglineHtml: Collected sources → clear angle → structured content → Figma-ready visual output.
valueHtml: Keeps research, drafting and visual layout connected through structured content and human review.
problemHtml: Research, drafting and visual layout are usually disconnected, so content work has to be redone by hand each time.
workflowHtml: I built a pipeline that collects sources, finds the angle, structures the evidence into content blocks, then hands off structured content directly into Figma.
resultShortHtml: 188 logged pipeline runs later, human review still gates every piece before it's approved or posted.
whatItProvesHtml: I can connect source collection, analysis and visual communication.
productionSignalHtml: Source material → angle finding → structured content blocks → Figma-ready output · human approval before publish
keyLabel: logged pipeline records · 50-topic batch runs · 2.5 months of operation
goalHtml:
• Turn collected sources into readable visual communication.
  • Connect research, analysis, content structure and visual output.
  • Avoid AI-invented facts.
  • Make repeated content work easier to review and maintain.
  • Create Figma-ready material from source-backed content.
methodHtml:
• Start from source material, not blank prompting.
  • Find the angle before drafting.
  • Structure evidence into content blocks.
  • Use AI to organise and draft, not to invent.
  • Use Figma as the visual handoff layer.
resultHtml:
• Built a repeated content workflow from source collection to visual output: 188 pipeline scripts.
  • Connected notes, references, angle finding, AI drafting and design handoff.
  • Prepared content for Figma-ready visual structure.
  • Kept human review before publication.
failureHandledHtml:
• Source comes before caption.
  • Weak evidence blocks strong visual claims.
  • Visual polish cannot hide missing sources.
  • Human review checks meaning, fact, caption and publication.
decisionHtml:
• AI helps structure and draft.
  • Human approves the final meaning.
  • Figma is the output layer, not the source of truth.
  • The workflow supports communication; it does not replace editorial responsibility.
limitationHtml:
• Some source evaluation remains manual.
  • Visual quality still depends on design judgment.
  • The system supports content production, not fully automated publishing.
  • More real screenshots should be added as proof captures.
stagesLabel: Workflow — source to angle to visual output

stages.title: Source collection
stages.body: Collect notes, images, references and source material from reviewed sources.
stages.caption: Tiered source material entering the pipeline.

stages.title: Angle finding
stages.body: Identify the main point before generating content or layout.
stages.caption: AI draft with a clear angle, before human review.

stages.title: Content structure
stages.body: Turn evidence into captions, content blocks and visual text.
stages.caption: Human review gate before anything is approved.

stages.title: Figma-ready output
stages.body: Prepare structured content for Figma layout and final human review.
stages.caption: Final Figma-ready visual output.

transferItems: Turns reviewed source material into visual output without losing evidence.
Connects database content to Figma-ready communication materials.

title: AI Output Review & Debug Memory System
shortTitle: AI Output Review & Debug Memory System
taglineHtml: Checks AI output, captures mistakes and turns recurring issues into reusable review rules.
valueHtml: Turns AI mistakes and unclear outputs into reusable review rules.
problemHtml: Confident-sounding AI output can hide missing evidence or overstated claims.
workflowHtml: I built a two-layer check — a rule-based gate plus an independent phrase scan — that reviews AI output before it's trusted, and logs what it finds.
resultShortHtml: An unsafe note was rejected, a clean one passed, and a real scanner bug was found, fixed and logged as a reusable lesson.
whatItProvesHtml: I can test AI output before trusting it, and turn bugs or unclear answers into reusable workflow improvements.
productionSignalHtml: Unsafe output rejected · clean output passed · scanner bug found and fixed · mistake logged for future reuse
keyLabel: independent safety layers · 1 real bug found and fixed
goalHtml:
• Stop AI output from being trusted too quickly.
  • Separate facts, assumptions, missing information and unsupported claims.
  • Catch overconfident or action-biased wording.
  • Record bugs and unclear outputs as reusable learning.
  • Improve future workflows through reviewed experience.
methodHtml:
• Start from rough AI output, workflow notes, bugs or unclear answers.
  • Extract the claim structure before judging the answer.
  • Check whether claims are supported by source material.
  • Run a second scan for risky wording or overclaiming.
  • Save the lesson into debug memory for future review.
resultHtml:
• Built an AI output review / decision-gate workflow.
  • Unsafe output was rejected.
  • Clean output passed.
  • A real scanner bug was found and fixed.
  • The bug was recorded as a reusable system lesson.
failureHandledHtml:
• Prevents confident AI output from becoming trusted output.
  • Flags missing evidence and unsupported claims.
  • Catches action-biased wording before reuse.
  • Keeps human interpretation separate from AI classification.
  • Treats bugs as material for system improvement.
decisionHtml:
• AI can classify, extract and flag risk.
  • AI does not decide what the evidence means.
  • Human review owns interpretation.
  • Bugs are not hidden; they become rule candidates.
  • The system learns through reviewed experience, not automatic self-modification.
limitationHtml:
• This is a human-reviewed learning loop, not a fully autonomous self-learning agent.
  • Rule-based checks can miss differently worded unsafe claims.
  • Human review is still required.
  • More real cases and proof captures should be added over time.
stagesLabel: Workflow — check AI output, then log the lesson

stages.title: Structured extraction
stages.body: Split rough AI output into facts, assumptions, missing information and claims.
stages.caption: Facts, interpretation and missing data split apart.

stages.title: Evidence and consistency check
stages.body: Checks whether claims have source support, whether key information is missing, and whether the answer overstates certainty.
stages.caption: Missing evidence and unsupported claims flagged.

stages.title: Independent phrase scan
stages.body: Runs a second check on the rendered output to catch unsafe wording, action bias or unsupported conclusions.
stages.caption: A second, separate scan of the rendered report text.

stages.title: Debug memory update
stages.body: Records the bug, lesson and what to avoid next time so the same mistake can become a future check.
stages.caption: REJECT or PASS_FOR_REVIEW, with reasons.

transferItems: Separates verified information from AI interpretation before reuse.
Uses two checks so one weak gate does not decide alone.

title: Method of Loci — automated document-ingestion pipeline
keyLabel: AI extracts · 175 posts · 748 assets · 3.5 months hands-off
goalHtml: Turn saved social-media archives into a searchable, offline knowledge base.
methodHtml: A scheduled Python pipeline imports, normalizes, AI-extracts, syncs and stores each item with status tracking.
resultHtml: Processed 175 posts, 748 media assets, 860 AI extracts and 162 Notion syncs.

stages.title: ZIP inbox
stages.body: Export archives enter a watched folder.

stages.title: Import + normalize
stages.body: Files are unpacked, renamed and registered.

stages.title: AI extraction
stages.body: Gemini writes searchable image and video descriptions.

stages.title: Sync to Notion
stages.body: Each post carries a pipeline status.

stages.title: Searchable database
stages.body: SQLite remains the offline source of truth.

title: AI-Assisted Video Pipeline — “decide before render”
keyLabel: Published productions · locked reusable template · full chain incl. privacy pass
goalHtml: Turn raw phone footage into publishable vertical reels without wasting time on repeated renders.
methodHtml: Plan, triage and approve the edit before rendering; compute is spent only after a human-approved plan.
resultHtml: Built a reusable template, then completed a second production with triage, voiceover, music bed, privacy pass and export.
stagesLabel: Workflow — plan approved before compute is spent

stages.title: Footage ingest
stages.body: Read metadata and group clips.

stages.title: Privacy pass
stages.body: Mosaic bystander faces with a QA contact sheet.

stages.title: Triage + EDIT_PLAN
stages.body: Mark KEEP / TRIM / KILL and write the edit plan.

stages.title: Human approval
stages.body: No render before sign-off.

stages.title: Build the cut
stages.body: ffmpeg assembles the approved plan.

stages.title: Final export
stages.body: Exported, metadata-stripped, versioned output.

title: Blender + ComfyUI — controllable AI video on free local models
keyLabel: Finished content tracks · graded masters · written case study
goalHtml: Test whether local AI video tools can produce controllable motion, camera and material effects.
methodHtml: Use Blender greybox scenes as control layers, then pass depth and reference images into local video generation.
resultHtml: Validated the workflow through 3 finished content tracks with graded masters, release formats and a written case study.
stagesLabel: Workflow — a 3D control layer feeds the video model

stages.title: Blender greybox
stages.body: Rough 3D scene defines camera and blocking.

stages.title: Depth render
stages.body: Depth pass becomes the control signal.

stages.title: Wan2.1 VACE generation
stages.body: Local model generates motion from control inputs.

stages.title: Grade + upscale
stages.body: Improve the raw output.

stages.title: Finish
stages.body: Add interpolation and room tone.

stages.title: Release packages
stages.body: Export vertical, square and showcase formats.
